folio.label: 45r
folio.l: With bred and chese , and good ale in a Iubbe
Suffisyng right I now , as for a day
But er that he , had mad al this array
He sent his knaue , and eke his wenche also
Vp on his nede , to londou~ for to go
And on the monday , whan it drow to nygút
He shette his dore , with outen candel ligút
And dressyd al thyng´ , as it shulde be
And shortly , vp they clymben alle thre
They setyn stille , wel a forlong wey
Now pater noster , clum seide Nicholay
And clum q¶ Ioúnö , and clum seide Alisouö
This Carpentä , seide his deuociou~
And stille he syt , and biddeth his prayere
A waytyng on the reyn , if he it here
The dede slepe , for verrey besynesse
Fel on this Carpenter , rigút as I gesse
A boute curfewe tyme , or litel more
For tuaille of his gost , he gronetú sore
And eft he routetú , for his hede myslay
Dounö on the laddre , stalketh Nicholay
And Alisou~ ful soft , a douö she spedde
With oute wordes mo , they gon to bedde
There as this Carpenter , is wont to lye
There was the reuel , and the melodye
And thus lyn Alisou~ , and Nicholas
In besynesse of myrthe , and in Solas
Til that the belle , of laudes gan to rynge
And freres in the chaunsel , gon~ synge
¢ This Parissú Clerk , this amerous Absolouö
That is for loue , al wey so woo bygonö
Vp on the monday , was at Osneye
With companye , hiö to disporte and pleye
And axed vp on cas , a cloisterere
Ful preuyly , after Ioún the Carpentere
And he drougú hi~ a pert , ou©t of the chirche
And seide I not , I saugú hi~ here nat werche
Sithe Satirday , I trowe æat he be went
For tymber , there oure Abbot hatú hi~ sent´
For he h h is wont , for tymber for to go
And dwellyn at the Graunge , a day or two
Or ellis he is , at his hous certeyn
Where æat he be , I kan nat sothely seyn
¢ This Absolou~ , ful Ioly was and ligút
And thou©t now is tyme , to wake al nygút
